lenses.guidance: The 'the LORD said to Moses' refrain begins in earnest here (vv 1, 8, 14, 19), structuring the plague cycle as a narrative of divine speech-act. Each plague follows the same pattern: word, sign, hardening, withdrawal.

Aaron's staff 'swallows' the magicians' staffs (v 12) — Hebrew bala', the same verb used at 1 Cor 15:54 ('death swallowed up in victory'). The pattern points to Christ's victory: divine power assimilates pretender power.
The first plague targets the Nile (vv 17-21) — Egypt's life-source and the river that carried Pharaoh's decreed deaths in 1:22. The redemptive judgement returns blood to the river that demanded it.

The plagues echo through Scripture — Joel 2 reuses locust imagery, Revelation 16's bowl-judgements directly cite the plague pattern (water-to-blood, frogs, sores). A canonical thread of judgement on rebellious empire.
